channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia bombards Ukraine's energy grid after Zelenskyy says his team will hold talks with the US
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia targeted Ukraine’s energy infrastructure in a large-scale missile and drone bombardment during the night, officials said Friday, hours after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said talks with the U.S. on ending the 3-year war will take place next week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Popular Iranian singer who urged women to go without hijab is lashed over alcohol conviction
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iranian officials have flogged a popular singer who posted a song online urging women to remove their mandatory headscarves over a conviction for possessing and consuming alcohol, his lawyer and authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former, active-duty Army soldiers charged in scheme allegedly selling sensitive military information to China
item.description: Three U.S. Army soldiers – two active-duty and one former – have been indicted for their alleged roles in gathering and selling sensitive information to China, the DOJ said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he’s 'looking at' revoking protected status for Ukrainians
item.description: “We're not looking to hurt anybody, and we're certainly not looking to hurt them,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: A Wisconsin judge has rejected state health officials’ attempt to keep a woman who stabbed her sixth-grade classmate to please horror character Slender Man more than a decade ago committed to a psychiatric hospital.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy Amphibious Base Coronado in San Diego County has been the subject of controversy for decades due to the exterior design of four barracks that resemble a swastika.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: A San Carlos Apache teen who was reported missing and then found dead marks one of the latest tragedies as Native American communities continue to grapple with the epidemic of people being killed or going missing at high rates.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Second measles death reported as outbreak grows in Southwest
item.description: The person was unvaccinated and a resident of Lea County, New Mexico, where at least 10 cases of the disease have been reported.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Gavin Newsom splits with Dems on trans athletes, prompting backlash from LGBTQ+ advocates
item.description: In a rebuke against the Democratic Party, California Gov. Gavin Newsom has revealed his thoughts on transgender athletes competing in girls' sporting events, and it has spurred reactions from across the state.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 men plead not guilty in drug overdose deaths of 3 Kansas City Chiefs fans after game
item.description: PLATTE CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two men pleaded not guilty Thursday in the drug overdose deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans whose bodies were found in a backyard two days after they got together to watch the final game of the regular season in 2024.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: California landlords prohibited from charging these fees starting in April
item.description: The bill also prohibits landlords or agents from charging a tenant any fee for paying rent or security deposits by check.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's PAC is running ads touting Trump's accomplishments
item.description: The political action committee that Elon Musk funded to the tune of more than $200 million on behalf of President Donald Trump launched a television advertisement this week lauding the first weeks of the administration in which the Tesla CEO is serving.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Roy Ayers,'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' musician, passes away at 84
item.description: Los Angeles native, Roy Ayers, who was behind the popular song "Everybody Loves the Sunshine" has passed away. He was 84 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Chipotle expands menus with new protein option
item.description: Chipotle customers can now customize their tacos, burritos, salads and quesadillas with a new protein option. The chain announced Thursday that it's adding Chipotle Honey Chicken to menus in the United States, Canada and the United Kingdom on March 7.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Migrants clone Border Patrol vehicles in bid to bypass Trump's crackdown
item.description: Some human smugglers have turned to cloning Border Patrol vehicles to bypass President Trump's crackdown on illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Denver mayor grilled over area’s Tren de Aragua problem as GOP lawmaker says policies to blame
item.description: Democrat Denver Mayor Mike Johnston is taking heat from state lawmakers over "weak immigration laws" and faced Congress over sanctuary status this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: House hunting surges where LA wildfires raged: Report
item.description: As Los Angeles residents recover from wildfires, house-hunting in many of the communities affected by the fires is reportedly surging.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tom Llamas to succeed Lester Holt as NBC "Nightly News" anchor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — NBC News appointed Tom Llamas on Wednesday to replace Lester Holt as anchor of “Nightly News” starting this summer, but he's not abandoning his streaming newscast. The elevation of Llamas, 45, was widely anticipated.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan brothers missing since 2010 declared dead at mom's request
item.description: A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gene Hackman's dog misidentified as other mysteries swirl around actor's death
item.description: Authorities misidentified a deceased dog while investigating the deaths of actor Gene Hackman and his wife, pianist Betsy Arakawa, according to a pet care specialist.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrat Al Green removed from House after interrupting, heckling Trump
item.description: Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) was removed from the House chamber where President Trump was giving his address to a joint session of Congress after the congressman heckled the president early in his speech.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Driver surrenders to police after pursuit full of PIT maneuvers, standoffs
item.description: A Los Angeles Police pursuit in the San Fernando Valley was full of starts and stops Tuesday afternoon, but the driver eventually surrendered to police.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4
